title: National Safety and Quality Health Service (NSQHS) Standards
description: The eight NSQHS Standards provide a nationally consistent framework for safety and quality in health service organisations. Mandatory for accreditation.
source: Australian Commission on Safety and Quality in Health Care

title: Aged Care Quality Standards
description: Eight standards that set out the level of care and services expected by aged care consumers. The basis for quality assessment and accreditation of aged care services.
source: Aged Care Quality and Safety Commission

title: Clinical Care Standards
description: Evidence-based statements describing the care patients should be offered for specific clinical conditions. Designed to reduce unwarranted variation.
source: Australian Commission on Safety and Quality in Health Care

title: NDIS Practice Standards
description: Quality standards for NDIS providers covering rights and responsibilities, governance, service delivery, and the service environment.
source: NDIS Quality and Safeguards Commission

title: ISO 31000 Risk Management
description: International standard providing principles, a framework, and guidelines for managing risk. Applicable to any organisation regardless of size or sector.
source: International Organization for Standardization

title: National Digital Health Strategy
description: Australia's framework for a digitally enabled health system — setting out priorities for secure, interoperable, and consumer-centred digital health.
source: Australian Digital Health Agency

title: National Framework for Digital Health Standards
description: Establishes a coordinated approach to digital health standards adoption in Australia, including interoperability, terminology, and clinical information standards.
source: Australian Digital Health Agency

title: FHIR (Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources)
description: The leading global standard for health care data exchange. FHIR defines how health information is structured, shared, and accessed across systems.
source: HL7 International

title: ELDAC Palliative Care Toolkit
description: Evidence-based resources supporting aged care providers to deliver quality palliative care and end-of-life care within residential and community settings.
source: End of Life Directions for Aged Care (ELDAC)

title: Australian Health Performance Framework
description: National framework for reporting on health system performance across effectiveness, safety, accessibility, continuity, efficiency, and sustainability.
source: Australian Institute of Health and Welfare

title: Clinical Governance Framework
description: Guidance on establishing clinical governance structures including safety systems, clinical effectiveness, consumer engagement, and workforce capability.
source: Australian Commission on Safety and Quality in Health Care

title: Open Disclosure Framework
description: National framework for open communication with patients and carers following adverse events — covering the process, principles, and governance requirements.
source: Australian Commission on Safety and Quality in Health Care

title: Antimicrobial Stewardship Clinical Care Standard
description: Quality statements to guide appropriate antimicrobial use across health care settings, reducing resistance and improving patient outcomes.
source: Australian Commission on Safety and Quality in Health Care

title: Serious Incident Response Scheme (SIRS)
description: Framework requiring aged care providers to identify, record, manage, resolve, and report serious incidents — a key compliance obligation.
source: Aged Care Quality and Safety Commission

title: A New Aged Care Act — Summary of Key Changes
description: Overview of the new rights-based Aged Care Act, replacing the Aged Care Act 1997. Covers strengthened quality standards, provider obligations, and consumer rights.
source: Australian Government Department of Health and Aged Care

title: Royal Commission into Aged Care — Final Report
description: The landmark report with 148 recommendations for reforming aged care in Australia. Essential reading for providers navigating the reform landscape.
source: Royal Commission into Aged Care Quality and Safety

title: Interoperability Roadmap for Australian Healthcare
description: Strategic roadmap for achieving seamless, secure data sharing across the Australian health system using FHIR and national standards.
source: Australian Digital Health Agency

title: Strengthened Aged Care Quality Standards
description: The new strengthened standards replacing the current Aged Care Quality Standards, with greater emphasis on clinical care, food and nutrition, diversity, and the aged care workforce.
source: Aged Care Quality and Safety Commission

title: Quality Indicator Program (QI Program)
description: National program requiring residential aged care providers to collect and report quality indicator data across key care domains — driving transparency and continuous improvement.
source: Australian Government Department of Health and Aged Care

title: SNOMED CT (Clinical Terminology)
description: The international clinical terminology standard used for consistent, computable representation of clinical concepts across electronic health records and health information systems.
source: National Clinical Terminology Service (NCTS)

title: My Health Records Act 2012
description: The legislative framework governing Australia's My Health Record system — covering data access, privacy protections, provider obligations, and consumer controls.
source: Australian Government

title: Australian Charter of Healthcare Rights
description: Describes the rights of patients and consumers when accessing health care services in Australia — a foundational document for consumer-centred governance and accreditation.
source: Australian Commission on Safety and Quality in Health Care
